outcome_text: AI system security and resilience—as identified in the MAP function—are evaluated and documented.
scoring_text: Knowledge of AI system security and resilience evaluation, including assessing AI-specific attack surfaces, evaluating system resilience to adversarial inputs and operational disruptions, and documenting security posture against risks identified during the MAP function.
_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Knowledge of AI system security and resilience evaluation, including assessing AI-specific attack surfaces, evaluating system resilience to adversarial inputs and operational disruptions, and documenting security posture against risks identified during the MAP function.
reference_document_element_description: Conduct threat model assessments for AI systems, identifying AI-specific attack surfaces, threat actors, and applicable mitigating controls.
comments: NIST AI RMF MS-2.7 (MEASURE) equal WIDAI RM domain. RM-T-002 is the strongest match (STS: 0.798, cosine: 0.701). Secondary: RM-S-002 (RM, cosine: 0.699), AG-K-003 (AG, cosine: 0.652).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression. Primary method for all elements. Scores the competency_implication text (workforce competency interpretation of the risk management outcome) against WIDAI KSA statements.
primary_method.significance: STS 8.0/10 (raw 0.798). Shared vocabulary: [ai, attack, specific, surfaces]. NIST AI RMF competency implication terms [adversarial, against, assessing, disruptions, documenting, evaluating] vs WIDAI terms [actors, applicable, assessments, conduct, controls, identifying] define the semantic distance.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.901 (P=0.889, R=0.914). R exceeds P by 0.025 — the WIDAI KSA's 17 words are well-captured by the competency implication's 33 words, but the competency implication contains broader scope.

nli_cross_encoder.description: Natural Language Inference classification. NLI entailment is systematically low for competency→competency pairs because NLI measures strict logical implication, not semantic relatedness. Primary diagnostic value is in detecting contradiction.
nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: notable entailment (54.7%). "Knowledge of AI system security and resilience evaluation, including assessing A" shows logical implication toward "Conduct threat model assessments for AI systems, identifying AI-specific attack" — stronger than typical for cross-framework competency pairs, reinforcing the STS-based relationship.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.701 < STS 0.798 (gap -0.098). Cross-attention finds deeper alignment than independent encoding. The relationship depends on cross-text reasoning between [adversarial, against, assessing] and [actors, applicable, assessments] that bi-encoders structurally cannot capture.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 12.8%. Shared tokens: [ai, attack, specific, surfaces]. NIST AI RMF-only: [adversarial, against, assessing, disruptions, documenting, evaluating]. WIDAI-only: [actors, applicable, assessments, conduct, controls, identifying].
